perturbation.original_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of three (3 ) years from the effective date of thi s Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of one (1) year from the effective date of this Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 4 reduces the confidentiality period for Proprietary Information from three years to one year. This creates a contradiction with Section 10(a) which refers to paragraph 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 4 stipulated a three-year confidentiality period. The changed text reduces this to one year. Section 10(a) explicitly states that the obligations in Paragraph 4 remain in effect for the term specified therein, and Section 10(b) requires data to be maintained for three years following the termination of the agreement, further reinforcing the original three-year commitment. Reducing the confidentiality period to one year in Section 4 directly contradicts Section 10, creating a clear and significant inconsistency regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary In formation to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will be confirmed within fifteen (15) days following initial disclosure by a written communication stating at least the date and circumstances under which th e disclosure occurred and the general nature of the information disclosed. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the disclosing Party with a suitable legend (such as "Pro prietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to pro tect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Disclosure of Proprietary Information to a receiving Party may be either oral or in writing. If an oral disclosure occurs, it will be confirmed within thirty (30) days following initial disclosure by a written communication stating at least the date and circumstances under which the disclosure occurred and the general nature of the information disclosed. When a writing contains Proprietary Information the writing will, prior to disclosure to the receiving Party, be marked by the receiving Party with a suitable legend (such as "Proprietary Information") to indicate its Proprietary status. The Parties shall utilize no less than reasonable care in protecting Proprietary Information received from the disclosing party; such care shall be the same degree of care as though it were to protect its own confidential or proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosing Party' to 'receiving Party' for marking the written Proprietary Information creates a conflict. The original text clearly states the disclosing party's obligation, while the altered version puts this obligation on the receiving party. This contradicts with the rest of the document that assumes disclosing party owns the propeitary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "PROPRIETARY Information" shall mean, in the case of Excelerate and the Second Party, proprietary or confidential information that is owned or controlled by each party relating to any solicitation or request for proposal . Each Party's P roprietary Information may include, but is not limited to, patents, copyrights, design methods, ideas, concepts, data, formulas, manufacturing techniques, know -how, business plans, customer lists, solicitation response strategies, technical solutions to cl ient requirements, system architectures, proposal preparation techniques and pricing policies, software, methodologies, technologies, processes, financial information, and sales and marketing information. Edit the highlighted portion of the first sentence in this paragraph to reflect the activities that are part of the NDA then delete this sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the responsibility for marking Proprietary Information from the disclosing party to the receiving party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'PROPRIETARY Information' as information owned or controlled by each party. If the receiving party is responsible for marking Proprietary Information, it creates a conflict with the idea that the disclosing party owns and controls that information. This creates an ambiguity and potential unenforceability regarding who is ultimately responsible for identifying and protecting Proprietary Information, as it is not the receiving party's information to begin with. The change aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return all Proprietary Information except that one copy may be retained by legal counsel of the receiving Party as evidence of what was disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Upon written request of a Party who has disclosed Proprietary Information to a receiving Party, the receiving Party shall return all Proprietary Information within 30 days of the request. No copies can be retained.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the exception allowing the receiving party's legal counsel to retain a copy of the Proprietary Information. This creates a contradiction with Section 10(b), which explicitly mentions that one copy is retained by the legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in paragraph 8 explicitly states that 'No copies can be retained' of the Proprietary Information. This directly contradicts section 10(b), which states 'except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.' The changed text in paragraph 8 nullifies the exception mentioned in 10(b), creating a clear contradiction. The original text in paragraph 8 did allow for one copy to be retained by legal counsel, solidifying the contradiction. This creates an ambiguity in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement shall be e ffective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate at the end of the period of years as provided for in paragraph 4 above, or upon the delivery of written notice of termination by a Party to the other Party; however, the obligations of a receiving Party pursuant to Paragraph 4 shall remain in effect for the term specified therein. b. Notwithstanding the termination of this agreement, the supplied data must be maintained and protected in accordance with its provisions fo r three (3) years following the termination of this agreement. At the conclusion of this agreement/contract for which data is exchanged, proprietary data shall be returned to the provider or destroyed with a certification to that effect provided to the ot her party, except the one (1) copy retained by legal counsel as provided in paragraph 8 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement shall be effective on the date of its full execution by the Parties. a. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon written notice from either Party to the other Party. b. Upon termination of this agreement, all supplied data must be immediately returned to the provider. There is no further obligation to maintain or protect the data.
perturbation.explanation: This changes Section 10 to allow immediate termination and remove any further data protection obligations. This contradicts Section 4, which states that Proprietary Information must be held in confidence for a specified period (originally three years, now changed to one year), even after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Except as provided in Paragraphs 6 and 7 hereof, Proprietary Information disclosed to a receiving Party shall for a period of one (1) year from the effective date of this Agreement be held in confidence by the receiving Party and not be disclosed to others or used except for the purposes set forth above, without the prior written approval of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 10 allows immediate termination and removes any further data protection obligations, stating 'There is no further obligation to maintain or protect the data'. This directly contradicts Section 4, which stipulates that Proprietary Information must be held in confidence for one (1) year from the effective date of the agreement. The original text in Section 10 maintained data protection obligations even after termination, ensuring consistency with Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Except as specifically provided herein, neither Party makes any warranty, express or implied, oral or written, with respect to products, services, or information supplied hereunder, including any warranties related to patent, trademark, or copyright infringements. In no event shall either Party be liable to the other for indirect, special, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or incidental damages including, but n ot limited to damages for loss of use of facilities or equipment, loss of revenue, loss of profits or loss of goodwill regardless of (a) the negligence (either sole or concurrent) of either party; and (b) whether either party has been informed of the possi bility of such damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Except as specifically provided herein, Excelerate makes no warranty, express or implied, oral or written, with respect to products, services, or information supplied hereunder, including any warranties related to patent, trademark, or copyright infringements. In no event shall Excelerate be liable to the other for indirect, special, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or incidental damages including, but not limited to damages for loss of use of facilities or equipment, loss of revenue, loss of profits or loss of goodwill regardless of (a) the negligence (either sole or concurrent) of either party; and (b) whether either party has been informed of the possibility of such damages. The Second Party warrants they have the right to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 11, The Second Party now warrants they have the right to disclose, but Excelerate does not provide a similar warranty. This creates an ambiguity because in Section 2, both parties are said to have this right. It is contradiction the overall fairness of the NDA's terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Each Party represents that to the best of its knowledge it has the right to disclose its Proprietary Information to the other without conflict with, or violati on of the rights of, any third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party makes any warranty regarding the right to disclose. The changed text adds that 'The Second Party warrants they have the right to disclose.' This directly contradicts Section 2, which states, 'Each Party represents that to the best of its knowledge it has the right to disclose its Proprietary Information to the other without conflict with, or violati on of the rights of, any third party.' The modified Section 11 now creates an inconsistency by affirming that only the Second Party warrants this right, while Section 2 implies both parties do, causing ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to th e disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Both parties hereby waiver any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of m onetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. The parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Proprietary Information, and breach of this agreement by the receiving party would cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party and that the disclosing party shall therefore be entitled to equitable relief in addition to all other remedies at law. The parties agree that, in the event of breach, or threatened breach of the terms of this agreement, the originating party may seek an injunction prohibiting such breach. Only the disclosing party hereby waives any requirement to post bond for attempts to obtain such injunctive relief. Any such relief shall be in addition to and not in lieu of any appropriate relief in the way of monetary damages. Each party shall pay its own attorney’s fees, costs, and expenses for any dispute under the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 14 removes the Second Party's waiver to post bond for injunctive relief, but keeps Excelerate's waiver. This changes the conditions for seeking equitable relief, and leads to unfair advantage, given both parties are meant to mutually agree with the terms of the contract. The inequity stems from the fact that both parties are giving up bond postings for breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT THIS NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (hereinafter referred to as the " Agreement ") is entered into by and between Excelerate, Inc. a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Alabama , (“Excelerate ”) having its principal place of business at 1230 Slaughter Road, Suite F, Madison, AL 35758 U.S.A., and Burton Technical Group ("Second Party "), with its corporate office located at Company Address and each or both of which shall also hereinafter be referred to as the " Party " or " Parties ," respectively. WHEREAS , the Parties represent that they control or may in the future control and have in their possession or may in the future possess valuable pro prietary, confidential information as described in Paragraph 1 of this Agreement; WHEREAS , in order for the Parties each to evaluate its interest in participating in a future business relationship it appears necessary that the Parties disclose to each othe r certain information; and WHEREAS , the Parties are willing to disclose and receive such information pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and neither Party has an obligation to supply PROPRIETARY Information; NOW, THEREFORE , the Parties agree as follows: 1. "PROPRIETARY Information" shall mean, in the case of Excelerate and the Second Party, proprietary or confidential information that is owned or controlled by each party relating to any solicitation or request for proposal . Each Party's P roprietary Information may include, but is not limited to, patents, copyrights, design methods, ideas, concepts, data, formulas, manufacturing techniques, know -how, business plans, customer lists, solicitation response strategies, technical solutions to cl ient requirements, system architectures, proposal preparation techniques and pricing policies, software, methodologies, technologies, processes, financial information, and sales and marketing information. Edit the highlighted portion of the first sentence in this paragraph to reflect the activities that are part of the NDA then delete this sentence.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 14 states that both parties waive the requirement to post bond for injunctive relief. The changed text modifies this by stating that only the disclosing party waives this requirement. This creates an inequity and contradicts the underlying principle of mutual agreement outlined in the NDA's preamble, specifically the WHEREAS clauses, which state that both parties are willing to disclose and receive information pursuant to the terms of the agreement. The change introduces an imbalance, giving one party (Excelerate) an advantage over the other, thus contradicting the spirit and intent of a mutually agreed-upon NDA.
